terms.definition: The plain-language goal of a visualization—e.g. compare groups, show distribution, reveal trend—stated before selecting a chart family.
A chart focused on one variable at a time, such as a histogram for a numeric column or a bar chart for category counts.
A chart relating two variables, typically a scatter plot for two numeric features or a box plot for numeric values grouped by category.
An x-axis with meaningful sequence—time, epoch, training step—where position implies progression and line charts are appropriate.
The visual pattern of a numeric variable—skew, modality, outliers, range—usually inspected with a histogram before modeling assumptions.
Color-encoded grid of values, such as a correlation or confusion matrix heatmap, used when the data is inherently two-dimensional tabular structure.
